items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled, pushing back the project at 333 Franklin Street to an unspecified later date. Residents who wanted to weigh in on this development will need to wait for a new hearing date to be announced.

items.headline: Major Tech Tenant Improvement at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4M commercial buildout at 1768 Automation Parkway suggests significant expansion by a tech or industrial tenant in San José's employment hub. This is among the largest commercial permits issued recently and indicates continued investment in the city's core business districts.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to its consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This ongoing project aims to improve traffic and safety at the Bascom Avenue crossing.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The city is developing an Active Transportation Plan (Phase 2) that will guide how Cupertino prioritizes bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and transit improvements. The revised scoring criteria will determine which projects get built first, directly affecting how residents can move around the city without cars.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract for unnamed project
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution to approve project plans, specifications, and award a construction contract, though the agenda does not specify which project. Residents should seek details on what infrastructure or development this contract funds.

items.headline: Milpitas amends housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 budget, which typically affects housing goals, federal funding priorities, and community development projects in Milpitas.

items.headline: New 6-Plex Under Construction on Seely Avenue
items.summary: Trimble Summerhill is building a 6-unit multi-family complex for $2.1M, adding modest housing supply in an area with limited recent residential development. Multi-family projects remain rare enough in San José to signal developer confidence in the market.

items.headline: City Council approves emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: The council voted on a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1, which will affect how the city locates temporary housing solutions. This policy change could influence where homeless encampments or temporary shelters are established throughout San José.
